messages.text: I used ChatGPT 5.1 and, for the first time, I didn’t have to correct anything in the code myself — it just worked. (Usually things get messed up again when I ask for further additions.)

messages.author: olanwe
messages.text: I also thought about using this approach for other firmware types, especially repeaters. You could preconfigure the basic connection settings and password via the patcher and handle the rest over the air. Since some people are migrating hard-to-reach Meshtastic repeaters to Meshcore without physical access, this could be really useful.

messages.author: olanwe
messages.text: OK, I had some discussions with the "AI guy" and now the option to create a "merged" image is available. I tested the option on my own companion and it did work without any issues so far. So instead of flashing a default or separate "merged" firmware first, it should be sufficient to activate the "Create image for first installation (merged)" switch and flash the resulting binary on the new device. If anyone runs into problems or notices anything unusual, please let me know. (edited)
messages.content_html: OK, I had some discussions with the "AI guy" and now the option to create a "merged" image is available. I tested the option on my own companion and it did work without any issues so far. So instead of flashing a default or separate "merged" firmware first, it should be sufficient to activate the "Create image for first installation (merged)" switch and flash the resulting binary on the new device. If anyone runs into problems or notices anything unusual, please let me know. (edited)

messages.author: olanwe
messages.text: It turned out that the variety of ESP boards is the real problem here. I would have to provide multiple bootloaders and then guess which one matches the uploaded firmware – which is unsafe and doesn’t make much sense. So I now also provide prebuilt merged binaries for fresh installations. However, the patcher can still extract the app binary from a merged image in case someone changes their mind at the last minute.
messages.content_html: It turned out that the variety of ESP boards is the real problem here. I would have to provide multiple bootloaders and then guess which one matches the uploaded firmware – which is unsafe and doesn’t make much sense. So I now also provide prebuilt merged binaries for fresh installations. However, the patcher can still extract the app binary from a merged image in case someone changes their mind at the last minute.

messages.author: olanwe
messages.text: Thanks for your feedback. Yes, I admit this is a bit confusing at the moment. The merged firmware contains the full image (including the bootloader) and is meant for initial flashing. For updates (i.e. if Meshcore is already running on the device), only the regular firmware is required (see readme.md). The “app only” switch can be used to extract the regular firmware from a merged image (in case you only have the merged version available but still want to perform an update). In normal use, this switch can simply be ignored. At the moment, the separation between patcher and firmware source is intentional, as it keeps things simple (at least on my side — I don’t have to implement an additional file selector and downloader). Since @recrof is already considering an implementation in the regular web flasher , I didn’t put too much effort into this functionality, as the patcher will likely become obsolete once that feature is available. I will add a version number and “dev” tag to the firmware files to make them easier to recognize among already downloaded files.
messages.content_html: Thanks for your feedback. Yes, I admit this is a bit confusing at the moment. The merged firmware contains the full image (including the bootloader) and is meant for initial flashing. For updates (i.e. if Meshcore is already running on the device), only the regular firmware is required (see readme.md). The “app only” switch can be used to extract the regular firmware from a merged image (in case you only have the merged version available but still want to perform an update). In normal use, this switch can simply be ignored. At the moment, the separation between patcher and firmware source is intentional, as it keeps things simple (at least on my side — I don’t have to implement an additional file selector and downloader). Since @recrof is already considering an implementation in the regular web flasher , I didn’t put too much effort into this functionality, as the patcher will likely become obsolete once that feature is available. I will add a version number and “dev” tag to the firmware files to make them easier to recognize among already downloaded files.

messages.reply_html:
olanwe
Thanks for your feedback. Yes, I admit this is a bit confusing at the moment. The merged firmware contains the full image (including the bootloader) and is meant for initial flashing. For updates (i.e. if Meshcore is already running on the device), only the regular firmware is required (see readme.md). The “app only” switch can be used to extract the regular firmware from a merged image (in case you only have the merged version available but still want to perform an update). In normal use, this switch can simply be ignored. At the moment, the separation between patcher and firmware source is intentional, as it keeps things simple (at least on my side — I don’t have to implement an additional file selector and downloader). Since @recrof is already considering an implementation in the regular web flasher , I didn’t put too much effort into this functionality, as the patcher will likely become obsolete once that feature is available. I will add a version number and “dev” tag to the firmware files to make them easier to recognize among already downloaded files.

messages.author: iHateComputers
messages.text: Hey Team, I hate to ask (not sure if this is even the right place to ask), but is anyone able to assist me with troubleshooting why my devices (Heltec V2 and V3) don't seem to work after flashing them with the "merged_patched.bin"? The flash seems to complete just fine, but then I get nothing on the OLED screen on the device and no new DHCP requests or Wi-Fi joins. Tested on both a Heltec V2 and V3. Flashing back to stock MC brings the device back to life. Any help would be greatly appreciated! Steps I'm performing: 1) Download merged file for initial device flash. Eg: "Heltec_v2_companion_radio_wifi_patchable-v1.11.0-dev-3c27132-merged.bin" 2) Key in Wi-Fi creds (triple checked for accuracy) into the patcher: https://www.weyhmueller.org/webtools/esp32_ssid_patcher.html 3) Press "Patch firmware" ("Save app-only when input is merged" is UNCHECKED) 4) Flash the firmware as a "custom" device in the official MC flasher web app ("Erase Device" CHECKED) 5) Wait for flash to complete. Logs indicate "100%, Hard resetting via RTS pin..." 6) Nothing, just a blank screen. Wait a few min, reboot button and hard power cycle have no effect. Again, any help would be greatly appreciated!
messages.content_html: Hey Team, I hate to ask (not sure if this is even the right place to ask), but is anyone able to assist me with troubleshooting why my devices (Heltec V2 and V3) don't seem to work after flashing them with the "merged_patched.bin"? The flash seems to complete just fine, but then I get nothing on the OLED screen on the device and no new DHCP requests or Wi-Fi joins. Tested on both a Heltec V2 and V3. Flashing back to stock MC brings the device back to life. Any help would be greatly appreciated! Steps I'm performing: 1) Download merged file for initial device flash. Eg: "Heltec_v2_companion_radio_wifi_patchable-v1.11.0-dev-3c27132-merged.bin" 2) Key in Wi-Fi creds (triple checked for accuracy) into the patcher: https://www.weyhmueller.org/webtools/esp32_ssid_patcher.html 3) Press "Patch firmware" ("Save app-only when input is merged" is UNCHECKED) 4) Flash the firmware as a "custom" device in the official MC flasher web app ("Erase Device" CHECKED) 5) Wait for flash to complete. Logs indicate "100%, Hard resetting via RTS pin..." 6) Nothing, just a blank screen. Wait a few min, reboot button and hard power cycle have no effect. Again, any help would be greatly appreciated!

messages.author: olanwe
messages.text: I think I know what is going wrong here. I guess the webflasher is unable to detect and handle custom merged-images at the moment, so it will flash the merged image as a regular app image. @recrof ? A workaround will be to flash an official firmware for the device first (e.g. the BLE one - you do not need to configure anything there!) and then flash the WiFi app-image (non-merged) afterwards. That should do the trick.
messages.content_html: I think I know what is going wrong here. I guess the webflasher is unable to detect and handle custom merged-images at the moment, so it will flash the merged image as a regular app image. @recrof? A workaround will be to flash an official firmware for the device first (e.g. the BLE one - you do not need to configure anything there!) and then flash the WiFi app-image (non-merged) afterwards. That should do the trick.
